subject: [PATCH v5 02/18] mm/huge_memory: preserve pmd_swp_uffd_wp on device-private PMD downgrade

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch fixes a bug in the Linux kernel's huge memory handling code, specifically when downgrading a device-private PMD (Page Map Directory) to a non-device-private one. The issue was that the pmd_swp_uffd_wp bit was being lost during this downgrade process, which could cause UFFD_WP (User Fault on File Descriptor Write Protection) traps to be bypassed. To fix this, the patch adds code to preserve the pmd_swp_uffd_wp bit when downgrading a device-private PMD.
